domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical home activities to support Noah's movement and physical skills, focusing on posture, strength, balance, coordination, and fine motor use. Each activity is designed to be short, repeatable, and suitable for his developmental level.
We do not think Noah has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
This activity bank provides a variety of language and communication-focused activities for Noah, designed to support his developmental needs while ensuring safety and practicality at home.
This activity bank provides a variety of cognitive and adaptive activities tailored for Noah, focusing on skill-building in a safe and engaging manner.

activities.title: Tummy Time with Elbow Support
activities.description: Place Noah on his tummy on a soft surface. Support his elbows under his shoulders to help him push up and hold his head steady. Encourage him to look around and reach for toys placed in front of him. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make different sounds like 'ma', 'ba', or 'ah' and encourage Noah to imitate you. Use a fun tone and facial expressions to engage him. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Supported Sitting Practice
activities.description: Sit Noah on the floor with pillows or a soft surface around him for support. Encourage him to lean on his hands while you place toys in front of him to reach for. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Turn-Taking Sounds
activities.description: Sit facing Noah and take turns making sounds. Start with a sound, then pause for him to respond. Celebrate his attempts to make sounds. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires.

activities.title: Toy Grasping and Reaching
activities.description: Place a variety of toys within Noah's reach while he is lying on his back. Encourage him to reach for and grasp the toys, helping him bring them to his mouth. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Keep the child upright and closely supervised. Avoid choking-risk foods and stop if coughing, gagging, or distress occurs.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Noah. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

We do not think Maya has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Maya's language and communication skills at home. Each activity is designed to be short, repeatable, and focused on enhancing her expressive and receptive language abilities.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, engaging activities designed to support Maya's cognitive and adaptive skills at home. Each activity is tailored to her developmental level and encourages exploration, imitation, and problem-solving.

activities.title: Follow the Leader
activities.description: Use simple commands like 'clap your hands' or 'touch your nose' and encourage Maya to follow along. Use gestures to model the actions.

activities.title: Block Stacking
activities.description: Provide Maya with a set of small blocks. Encourage her to stack at least two blocks on top of each other. You can model this by stacking the blocks yourself first.

activities.title: Name That Toy
activities.description: Hold up a familiar toy and clearly say its name. Encourage Maya to look at the toy when you say its name. Repeat with different toys.

activities.title: Chores Imitation
activities.description: Pick a simple chore, like sweeping with a broom. Show Maya how to do it and encourage her to imitate you. You can use a small broom for her to use.

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys out of reach. Encourage Maya to point at the toy she wants and say 'please' or 'help'. Respond by giving her the toy.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Maya. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities focused on movement and physical skills for Leo, supporting his development in a safe and engaging way.
This activity bank provides a variety of short, engaging activities designed to support Leo's social and emotional development, focusing on eye contact, shared attention, and social engagement.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Leo's language and communication skills at home. Each activity is designed to be short, predictable, and focused on enhancing communication through play and interaction.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Leo's cognitive and adaptive skills at home. Each activity is designed to be engaging, predictable, and suitable for his developmental level.

activities.title: Container Play
activities.description: Give Leo a container and a few small toys. Encourage him to put the toys in the container and take them out. You can model this action first. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Book Pointing Game
activities.description: Choose a simple picture book. Ask Leo to point to items in the book when you say their names, like 'Where's the bear?' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Dress-Up Game
activities.description: Play a game where you help Leo dress a doll or stuffed animal. Encourage him to push the arm through the sleeve or lift the foot to put on shoes. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: More or All Done
activities.description: During snack time, use simple phrases like 'more' or 'all done.' Encourage Leo to use these words to express his needs. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Point and Show
activities.description: Go outside or look out the window. Encourage Leo to point at interesting things he sees, and respond with excitement or questions to promote engagement. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Social / Emotional, Language / Communication for Leo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides a variety of movement and physical activities designed for Ethan, focusing on improving his self-care motor skills, fine motor hand use, and gross motor mobility in a safe and supportive environment.
We do not think Ethan has a meaningful delay in Social / Emotional and may not need special support in this category right now.
We do not think Ethan has a meaningful delay in Language / Communication and may not need special support in this category right now.

This activity bank includes six practical home activities designed to support Ethan's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and following directions. Each activity is tailored to his developmental level and safety needs.

activities.title: Bead Stringing
activities.description: Provide large beads and a shoelace or string. Encourage Ethan to thread the beads onto the string. Offer support by holding the string steady if needed. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather colored objects (like blocks or toys). Ask Ethan to sort them into groups by color. You can help him name each color as he sorts.

activities.title: Fork Practice
activities.description: Set up a small snack with cut-up soft fruits or vegetables. Encourage Ethan to use a fork to pick up the food. Provide guidance and support as needed. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

activities.title: Counting Fun
activities.description: Count objects around the house together (like toys or snacks). Encourage Ethan to count to 10 with your support, pointing to each item as you count.

activities.title: Ball Catching
activities.description: Sit on the floor with Ethan and use a large, soft ball. Roll the ball to him and encourage him to catch it. Use hand support if necessary to help him position his hands correctly. Use stable support and close adult supervision throughout.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Movement / Physical, Cognitive / Adaptive for Ethan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical language and communication activities for Amina, focusing on enhancing her receptive and expressive language skills while ensuring tasks are short and manageable.
This activity bank provides practical, short, and engaging activities to support Amina's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and following directions.

activities.title: Color Sorting
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or toys). Ask Amina to sort them by color into different containers. You can help her by naming the colors as she sorts. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Ball Toss
activities.description: Sit on the floor with Amina and roll a large ball back and forth. Encourage her to catch the ball when you roll it to her. Celebrate her successes! Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Simple Story Sequencing
activities.description: Use a familiar storybook. Read a page and then ask Amina what happens next. Encourage her to point to pictures that represent the story. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Crayon Drawing
activities.description: Provide Amina with crayons and paper. Encourage her to hold the crayon between her fingers and thumb while she draws simple shapes or lines. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Drawing Shapes
activities.description: Show Amina how to draw a circle. Then, encourage her to try drawing it herself. Praise her efforts and guide her if needed. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

domain_rows.summary: This activity bank provides practical home activities for Lucas, focusing on movement and physical skills appropriate for his developmental level. Each activity is designed to support his growth while considering his sensory sensitivities and need for short, manageable tasks.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical home activities designed to support Lucas's social and emotional development. Each activity focuses on enhancing social engagement and emotional regulation while considering his specific needs and preferences.

This activity bank provides engaging and practical activities to support Lucas's language and communication development at home. Each activity is designed to be short, repeatable, and tailored to his current level while also promoting his next steps in communication.
This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Lucas's cognitive and adaptive skills at home, focusing on exploration, problem-solving, and functional play.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make simple sounds like 'ahh', 'ooo', or 'ee' and encourage Lucas to imitate you. Use a mirror to make it fun and visually engaging. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Peek-a-Boo with a Twist
activities.description: Sit in front of Lucas and cover your face with a soft cloth. Then, reveal your face with a big smile and say 'Peek-a-boo!' Encourage him to mimic your expression. Repeat several times. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Turn-Taking Sounds
activities.description: Sit face-to-face with Lucas and take turns making sounds. Start with a sound, then wait for him to respond. Encourage him to make a sound back. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Facial Expression Imitation
activities.description: Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage Lucas to imitate you. Use a mirror if possible to help him see his own expressions. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

activities.title: Follow the Sound
activities.description: Make a sound (like clapping or a gentle bell) and see if Lucas turns his head towards the sound. Praise him when he does. Keep the activity in short bouts and pause for rest if the child tires. Use lower-noise, lower-clutter materials when possible.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Emma's language and communication skills, focusing on sound production, gestures, and following directions.

This activity bank includes six practical home activities designed to support Emma's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on following directions, problem-solving, and imitation through play. Each activity is short and manageable to prevent frustration and encourage engagement.

activities.title: Toy Naming Game
activities.description: Show Emma a toy and say its name clearly. Encourage her to repeat the name or make a sound related to the toy. Use gestures to help her understand. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Color Sorting Game
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or crayons). Ask Emma to find and hand you a specific color, like 'Can you give me the red one?'. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Follow the Simple Direction
activities.description: Give Emma a simple one-step direction like 'give me the toy' without gestures. Praise her when she follows the direction. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Emma a simple two-step instruction, such as 'Pick up the toy and put it in the box'. Praise her when she follows the instructions. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Point and Ask
activities.description: Place a few toys in front of Emma. Encourage her to point to a toy she wants and say its name or make a sound. Respond to her request. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

This activity bank provides a variety of short, practical activities designed to support Zain's cognitive and adaptive skills, focusing on attention, problem-solving, and following directions. Each activity is tailored to be engaging and manageable, considering Zain's developmental needs.

activities.title: Color Sorting Game
activities.description: Gather a few colored items (like blocks or crayons). Ask Zain to sort them by color into different containers. Start with two colors and gradually add more as he becomes comfortable. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Clothes Dressing Practice
activities.description: Lay out loose pants and a jacket. Encourage Zain to put them on by himself with your support as needed. Start with one item and gradually add another. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions
activities.description: Give Zain simple two-step instructions, such as 'Pick up the toy and put it in the box.' Praise him for following the directions. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Bead Stringing
activities.description: Provide large beads and a string. Show Zain how to string the beads together, encouraging him to try it independently. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Pretend Play with Dolls
activities.description: Use dolls or stuffed animals to engage in pretend play. Encourage Zain to feed the doll or put it to bed, modeling the actions for him to imitate. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Body Part Show and Tell

activities.description: Read a picture book together. Ask Isla to point to specific items in the pictures, like 'Where is the bear?' or 'Show me the car.' Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Two-Step Instructions Game
activities.description: Give Isla simple two-step instructions, like 'pick up the toy and put it in the box.' Praise her when she follows the directions correctly. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Fun
activities.description: Make animal sounds and encourage Isla to imitate them. Start with simple sounds like 'moo' or 'quack'. Celebrate her attempts with claps or cheers. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

Each activity is designed to be short, repeatable, and suitable for home, focusing on enhancing expressive and receptive language skills through fun interactions.
This activity bank provides practical home activities for Mateo to support his cognitive and adaptive skills. Each activity is designed to be engaging and suitable for his developmental level.

activities.title: Sound Imitation Game
activities.description: Make different animal sounds and encourage Mateo to imitate them. Start with simple sounds like 'moo' for a cow or 'meow' for a cat. Use toys or pictures of the animals to make it more engaging.

activities.title: Face-to-Face Play
activities.description: Sit on the floor facing Mateo. Make different facial expressions (happy, sad, surprised) and encourage him to imitate you. Use a mirror if available to enhance engagement.

activities.title: Follow the Gesture
activities.description: Use simple gestures like waving, pointing, or clapping and encourage Mateo to follow along. You can say 'wave bye-bye' or 'clap your hands' while demonstrating the action.

activities.title: Clean-Up Time Game
activities.description: Turn clean-up time into a game. Sing a clean-up song while picking up toys together. Encourage Mateo to help by responding to your prompts.

activities.title: Simple Direction Following
activities.description: Give Mateo simple directions using both words and gestures. For example, say 'Give me the ball' while holding out your hand. Praise him when he follows the direction.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional, Language / Communication, Cognitive / Adaptive for Mateo. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Luna's social and emotional development, focusing on engagement, interaction, and emotional regulation.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Luna's language and communication skills at home. Each activity is designed to be engaging and suitable for her developmental level, focusing on enhancing her expressive and receptive language abilities.

This activity bank provides practical, short activities to support Luna's cognitive and adaptive skills at home. Each activity is designed to be engaging and achievable within a few minutes, focusing on skill-building in a safe environment.

activities.title: Body Part Show and Tell
activities.description: Ask Luna to point to different body parts on herself or a doll when you name them. For example, say 'Where's your nose?' and encourage her to point.

activities.title: Clean-Up Time Together
activities.description: At clean-up time, say 'It's clean-up time!' and encourage Luna to help pick up toys. Make it fun by singing a clean-up song together.

activities.title: Book Pointing Game
activities.description: Choose a picture book and ask Luna to point to specific animals or objects when you ask, like 'Where is the bear?' or 'Show me the cat.'

activities.title: Look at Me Game
activities.description: Take turns showing each other something fun you can do, like jumping or spinning. Encourage Luna to say 'Look at me!' when it’s her turn.

activities.title: Two-Word Phrases
activities.description: Encourage Luna to use two-word phrases by modeling them. For example, say 'More juice' or 'Big truck' and prompt her to repeat after you.

activities.description: Take turns making faces to show different emotions (happy, sad, surprised). Ryan can guess the emotion and then mimic it back. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Feelings Storytime
activities.description: Read a short story together. Pause to discuss how the characters might feel and why. Encourage Ryan to express his thoughts. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Role Play Helpers
activities.description: Pretend to be helpers in different scenarios (like a doctor, teacher, or firefighter). Take turns helping each other and discussing how it feels to help. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Transition Countdown
activities.description: Use a timer to signal transitions between activities. When the timer goes off, practice saying goodbye to the current activity and hello to the next. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

activities.title: Compliment Circle
activities.description: Sit in a circle (or face each other) and take turns giving compliments. Encourage Ryan to think of something nice to say about you. Keep it short and predictable, offer a simple choice, and stop before frustration escalates.

summary_text: This plan prioritizes Social / Emotional for Ryan. The activities are intended to be short, parent-friendly, and aligned with the developmental areas that appeared most delayed or most worth enriching in the interview.
